steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: Between the noun “polymath” and the relative clause “who was not the first to identify the mathematical bound,” the phrase “in this case, Herman Chernoff” sits as a non-restrictive appositive clarifying which polymath is meant.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Non-restrictive appositives must be set off with matching punctuation marks.

Option A correctly uses symmetrical em-dashes: “polymath—in this case, Herman Chernoff—who was not the first...”.

Options B, C, and D violate the rule by using mismatched punctuation (colon-comma, em-dash-comma, and no opening mark, respectively).
That matches choice A — A is correct.
